title: NOC 22112 – Forestry Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Forestry Technologists and Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, driven by sustainable-forestry programs, wildfire-management strategies, timber harvesting operations, and conservation initiatives. Classified under NOC 22112 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Conduct forest inventories, surveys, and field measurements
Prepare forest-management and harvest plans using GIS and mapping tools
Lead silviculture programs (planting, thinning, site prep)
Monitor logging companies for regulatory compliance
Support forestry research and nursery operations
Develop forestry databases and technical reports
Provide public education and advisory services
employment_requirements: One- to three-year college diploma in forestry technology or renewable resources
Registration with provincial regulatory bodies (except in Prince Edward Island and Manitoba)
In Quebec, membership in the professional-technologists’ order is required to use protected titles
job_outlook: This favourable gap makes the occupation particularly attractive for candidates with:
additional_information: Canada’s forest sector continues to expand due to:
Wildfire-management programsSustainable-logging regulationsReforestation initiativesTimber-supply monitoringEnvironmental-protection enforcementIndigenous-land stewardship projects
Annual salaries typically range between $36,600–$79,200 CAD, depending on province, role, and experience.
